SEARCH
Package p2main

Class MunicipalAlgorithm

java.lang.Object
p2main.VotingAlgorithm
p2main.MunicipalAlgorithm
public class MunicipalAlgorithm extends VotingAlgorithm
Implements the Municipal voting algorithm. This voting system assigns votes to each candidate the voter picks. The candidate with the most votes wins. If a tie occurs, a random tie-breaking mechanism is used.

    • election

      public Election election
      The election object containing candidates and ballots.

      Stores the number of votes each candidate received.

  • Constructor Details

    • MunicipalAlgorithm

      public MunicipalAlgorithm(Election election)
      Constructor for MvAlgorithm.
      Parameters:
      election - the Election object that provides candidate and seat information.

  • Method Details

    • runAlgorithm

      public void runAlgorithm(List<Ballot> ballots)
      Executes the municipal voting algorithm: 1. Counts votes from ballots. 2. Determines the winners and losers. 3. Displays results.
      Specified by:
      runAlgorithm in class VotingAlgorithm
      Parameters:
      ballots - list of ballots cast in the election.

    • displayResults

      protected void displayResults()
      Displays the final election results, including vote totals for each winner and loser.
      Specified by:
      displayResults in class VotingAlgorithm

    • breakTie

      protected List<Integer> breakTie(List<Integer> tieList)
      Breaks a tie by randomly selecting a number of candidates equal to the number of remaining seats.
      Specified by:
      breakTie in class VotingAlgorithm
      Parameters:
      tieList - list of candidate indices tied at the cutoff vote count.
      Returns:
      list of selected candidate indices to break the tie.

    • getWinnerList

      public List<String> getWinnerList()
      Retrieves the list of winners from the election. This method returns a list of candidates who have won the election based on the results of the Plurality algorithm. The list may contain one or more candidates depending on the election results (e.g., in the case of ties).
      Returns:
      a list of candidate names who are the winners of the election.

    • getLoserList

      public List<String> getLoserList()
      Retrieves the list of losers from the election. This method returns a list of candidates who have lost the election based on the results of the Plurality algorithm. The list may contain one or more candidates depending on the election results.
      Returns:
      a list of candidate names who are the losers of the election.
